verified.name: Quotes on Design
verified.tagline: Design-world wisdom served from a WordPress API
verified.description: Pulls inspirational quotes about design and creativity from the Quotes on Design site's WordPress REST endpoint. Each call returns a post with the author's name and the quote as rendered HTML. No key needed, just refresh for another dose of studio wisdom.

content.rendered: A human being should be able to change a diaper, plan an invasion... Specialization is for insects.

verified.notes: Backed by the standard WordPress REST API (wp/v2/posts); orderby=rand is not supported, use a plain posts query.

verified.name: Stoicism Quote
verified.tagline: One Stoic maxim per request from a public Lambda API
verified.description: Returns a single random Stoic quote with its author, drawn from thinkers like Marcus Aurelius. It is backed by an open-source AWS Lambda project and answers plain GET requests with tidy JSON. Handy for calm-inducing widgets and daily-quote bots.

sampleJson.text: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together,but do so with all your heart.
sampleJson.author: Marcus Aurelius

verified.name: isEven (humor)
verified.tagline: The API that reveals whether a number is even
verified.description: A tongue-in-cheek service that takes a number in the URL path and tells you, in JSON, whether it is even. The free tier even bundles a random text advertisement in every response. Gloriously unnecessary and thoroughly keyless.

sampleJson.ad: SURGEON WANTED for a new heath clinic opening in SF. No experience needed. Must have own tools. Call 407-555-5400

verified.freeTier: Free tier limited to numbers 0–999,999 with ads (per site's joke pricing)
verified.notes: Parody API; the tiered 'pricing' on the site is a joke.

verified.name: ISRO
verified.tagline: India's spacecraft roster as clean JSON
verified.description: Lists spacecraft launched by the Indian Space Research Organisation, each with an id and name. This community-built endpoint wraps ISRO's public data behind a simple, CORS-friendly REST call. No key required to browse the fleet.

verified.name: TLE
verified.tagline: Two-line element sets for orbiting satellites
verified.description: Serves NORAD two-line element (TLE) orbital data for satellites by catalog number, including the familiar line1/line2 pair and a timestamp. Query 25544 and you get the ISS's current elements. Ideal for orbit-propagation and satellite-tracking projects.

verified.name: Lorem Picsum
verified.tagline: Placeholder photos, plus a JSON catalog of them
verified.description: Best known for serving random placeholder images, Lorem Picsum also exposes a JSON list endpoint describing available photos with author, dimensions, and download URLs. The images themselves come from Unsplash. Great for mocking up galleries without hardcoding assets.
verified.notes: Image endpoints return binary; /v2/list is the JSON catalog used here.

verified.name: inspirehep.net
verified.tagline: Search the high-energy-physics literature by API
verified.description: INSPIRE is the reference database for high-energy physics, and its REST API lets you query papers, authors, and citations. A literature search returns hit metadata like titles and author affiliations as JSON. Open access to millions of HEP records with no login.
verified.notes: Response includes X-RateLimit-* headers, so throttling is enforced but the numeric policy was not confirmed from docs.

verified.name: iDigBio
verified.tagline: Search millions of digitized biological specimens
verified.description: iDigBio aggregates natural-history specimen records from museums and collections worldwide. Its search API accepts a JSON record-query and returns matching specimens with full Darwin Core fields. A single query can surface hundreds of thousands of catalogued organisms.
verified.notes: First response was slow (~5.4s); rq is a URL-encoded JSON record query.

verified.name: arcsecond.io
verified.tagline: Astronomical object data from a single endpoint
verified.description: Arcsecond.io consolidates astronomy data sources behind one API, resolving object names to coordinates, aliases, and catalog cross-references. Ask for m1 and it returns the Crab Nebula's equatorial position and a stack of alternate designations. Keyless for basic object lookups.

verified.name: ITIS
verified.tagline: Federal taxonomy: names, authors, and TSNs
verified.description: The Integrated Taxonomic Information System is a U.S. government database of scientific names for plants, animals, fungi, and microbes. Its JSON web service matches a search term to taxa, returning each name's author, validity, and taxonomic serial number. Authoritative and free to query.
verified.notes: Served as text/json; no access-control-allow-origin header observed (CORS false). U.S. federal government data source.

verified.name: Newton
verified.tagline: Symbolic and arithmetic math over HTTP
verified.description: Newton solves math expressions passed in the URL path, handling operations like simplify, factor, derive, and integrate. Send it 2^2+2(2) to simplify and it replies with the result 8 in JSON. An open-source calculator you can call from anywhere.

verified.name: Noctua
verified.tagline: Resolve star and sky-object names to catalog data
verified.description: NoctuaSky's REST API powers a stargazing app and resolves celestial object names to detailed records. Look up Polaris and you get magnitudes, coordinates, spectral type, and a long list of catalog aliases. Useful for planetarium and observing tools.

verified.name: Open Science Framework
verified.tagline: Programmatic access to the Open Science Framework
verified.description: The OSF API exposes public research projects, files, and metadata from the Center for Open Science's platform. Listing nodes returns project titles, descriptions, categories, and timestamps as JSON:API documents. A gateway to open study designs, data, and preprints.
verified.notes: JSON:API (application/vnd.api+json); no access-control-allow-origin header observed on this response.

verified.name: SHARE
verified.tagline: An open dataset of scholarly research activity
verified.description: SHARE compiles metadata about research outputs and scholarly events from a broad network of sources. Its v2 API lists those harvest sources and exposes the aggregated records as JSON:API. A window into who is publishing and sharing what across academia.
verified.notes: JSON:API; status endpoint reports version 26.0.0, up.

skipped.name: They Said So Quotes
skipped.reason: Free quote-of-the-day endpoint (quotes.rest/qod) now returns 401 'Not authenticated'; requires an API key. The theysaidso.com quote path serves HTML, not JSON.

skipped.name: Traitify
skipped.reason: Requires credentials — /v1/decks returns 401 'Credentials are required'. No keyless JSON GET; personality-assessment API needs signup.

skipped.name: Screenshotlayer
skipped.reason: Requires an access_key (returns error 101 missing_access_key). No keyless endpoint.

skipped.name: Purple Air
skipped.reason: Requires an API key — /v1/sensors returns 403 ApiKeyMissingError.

skipped.name: PlaceKeanu
skipped.reason: Returns an image (image/svg+xml), not JSON. No JSON GET endpoint.

skipped.name: Readme typing SVG
skipped.reason: Returns an SVG image (image/svg+xml), not JSON. It is an image-generation service.

skipped.name: CodeCogs
skipped.reason: LaTeX-to-image renderer; the JSON endpoint returned text/plain 'Invalid option or file does not exist' on 2 attempts and its real output is images, not a JSON data response.

skipped.name: SpaceX
skipped.reason: REST API (api.spacexdata.com/v4) returns HTTP 525 (origin SSL handshake failed) on all 3 attempts — backend is down/unmaintained.

skipped.name: SpaceX
skipped.reason: GraphQL endpoint (api.spacex.land/graphql) now serves a WordPress HTML page; API is gone, and GraphQL would require a POST body anyway.

skipped.name: Times Adder
skipped.reason: Requires a POST with an array body to sum times; no keyless GET endpoint that returns JSON.
